mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.4pp, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that sustains corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with an accuracy exceeding 55%, or the interaction term loses explanatory power in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the industrial production index reflect immediate supply-side bottlenecks that trigger short-term equity sell-offs, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth does not precede negative 5-day SPX returns in >50% of cases, or the predictive power vanishes when CPIAUCSL growth remains below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding 3.5% forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, which increases discount rates and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5%, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market absorption that supports consumer spending and equity valuations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields a Rank IC below 0.01 over a 5-year sample, or fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP establishes the fundamental earnings trajectory for S&P 500 constituents, where higher GDP growth directly translates to positive forward equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns across multiple business cycles, or the signal reverses direction during liquidity crunches.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between industrial production growth and retail sales growth indicates inventory misalignment that triggers short-term market volatility and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The divergence condition fails to predict negative 5-day returns in >55% of triggered instances, or the mechanism loses predictive accuracy when FEDFUNDS remains unchanged.
